Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Correcting Clerical Error On Face Of Order After Hearing Entered December 22, 2021 On Plaintiff'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-09-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
KENNETH PRITIKIN, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF KENNETH PRITIKIN, (510) 710-7136, kwpritikin@gmail.com, for Plaintiff ESFIR VINER,
SARAH N. LEGER, ESQ., RAGGHIANTI FREITAS LLP, (415) 453-9433, for Other Party Kim Schwarcz, Personal Representative of the Estate of David J. McMahon,

PLAINTIFF BOGDAN VINER'S Motion For Order Correcting Clerical Error On Face Of Order After Hearing Entered December 22, 2021 On Plaintiff'S Motion For Summary Adjudication.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Bogdan Viner's ("Bogdan") motion for order correcting clerical error is denied. First, Bogdan seeks to remedy a purported judicial error rather than a clerical error. "The distinction between a clerical error and a judicial error does not depend so much on the person making it as it does on whether it was the deliberate result of judicial reasoning and determination." (Estate of Doane (1964) 62 Cal.2d 68, 71.) The court has no power "to set aside or amend judicial errors except under appropriate statutory proceedings." (Id.) Second, this motion is an untimely and substantively defective motion for reconsideration in violation of CCP 1008.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jun-09-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
KENNETH PRITIKIN, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF KENNETH PRITIKIN, (510) 710-7136, kwpritikin@gmail.com, for Plaintiff ESFIR VINER,
SARAH N. LEGER, ESQ., RAGGHIANTI FREITAS LLP, (415) 453-9433, for Other Party Kim Schwarcz, Personal Representative of the Estate of David J. McMahon,

PLAINTIFF BOGDAN VINER MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Bogdan Viner's motion for summary adjudication is denied.

Plaintiff fails to maintain his burden of production as to issue one. Plaintiff contends that defendants owed a contractual duty to pay him. But plaintiff does not plead a breach of contract cause of action and his separate statement of undisputed fails to set forth clearly the terms or the type of alleged contract between the parties. A motion for summary adjudication must be addressed to the claims or defenses raised in the pleadings. (See 580 Folsom Assoc. v. Prometheus Develop. Co. (1990) 223 Cal. App.3d 1, 14.)

Plaintiff fails to maintain his burden on production as to issue two. Plaintiff contends that defendants owed a statutory duty to treat plaintiff as an employee under Dynamex Operations West, Inc. v. Superior Court of Los Angeles (2018) 4 Cal.5th 903. But Dynamex does not apply to plaintiff's second cause of action for waiting time penalties. (See Garcia v. Border Transportation Group, LLC (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 558, 571 [holding after the Dynamex decision: "Garcia's claims for overtime, wrongful termination, waiting time penalties, and UCL claims resting on the foregoing are governed by the common law test articulated in Borello."].) Since plaintiff failed to address the test applicable to the second cause of action, the burden did not shift. (See S.G. Borello & Sons, Inc. v. Department of Industrial Relations (1989) 48 Cal. 3d 341.)

Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 3 -6. Plaintiff failed to maintain her initial burden of production per CCP 437(p)(1). Plaintiff does not clearly set forth the total amount of her claimed damages.

The court notes that defendants refer to the Leger declaration, but it was never filed with the court.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
